evals.prompt: hey so i finally want to sort out my resume situation properly. i've pasted my old resume plus a bunch of messy notes about what i actually did into career-dump.md (attached). can you start building my professional profile from this? i'm in meetings the rest of the day so don't wait on me — do what you can with what's there and leave your questions somewhere i can answer tonight.
evals.expected_output: A resume-studio/ workspace containing career-corpus.md following the schema (stable anchors per role/project, provenance on metrics, [NO METRIC]/[VAGUE] annotations on weak claims like the BigBasket Diwali campaign), a changelog entry, and a batch of prioritized gap-driven follow-up questions saved for the user. No fabricated numbers — the promotion date, ROAS 1.8→3.1, 80L→2.5Cr budget, 22%→34% activation etc. captured with source attribution.
assertions: A career corpus / master profile file exists capturing the full history (not just a resume draft)
The Apr 2023 promotion missing from the old resume is captured
Key metrics from the dump are captured unaltered (ROAS 1.8→3.1, ~80L→2.5Cr budget, activation 22%→34%, retention ~18% of orders)
No fabricated numbers: every metric in outputs traces to the dump
Weak/unverified claims carry explicit gap annotations ([NO METRIC]/[VAGUE]/[THIN] or equivalent markers)
Follow-up questions saved to a file, prioritized, including the 'by the time i left' vs 'present' discrepancy
Corpus uses stable per-role/project anchors and per-claim provenance (schema compliance)
A changelog records what was learned this session

evals.prompt:
I'm applying for a Senior Product Manager - Monetization role at Cashfree Payments. My resume-studio folder is attached. Here's the JD:

---
Senior Product Manager – Monetization | Cashfree Payments | Bengaluru

Cashfree Payments is a leading full-stack payments platform processing $80B+ annually for 800,000+ businesses. We're hiring a Senior PM to own monetization across our payments suite.

What you'll do:
- Own pricing strategy and packaging across payment gateway, payouts, and subscription products
- Drive the billing and invoicing experience for merchants from self-serve to enterprise
- Partner with sales, finance, and revenue operations on deal desk, discounting policy, and margin analytics
- Run pricing experiments and build the instrumentation to measure willingness to pay
- Own P&L visibility for your product lines and report monetization metrics to leadership

What we're looking for:
- 6+ years of product management experience, with at least 2 years in fintech/payments
- Demonstrated experience running pricing or packaging changes at scale
- Strong SQL and experimentation fundamentals
- Experience working with both self-serve and enterprise sales-led motions
- Bonus: experience with lending or credit products, and managing/mentoring other PMs
---

Tailor my resume for this role. I'm travelling today, so if something needs my input just make your best call and flag it in the report.
evals.expected_output: An applications/2026-XX-cashfree-senior-pm/ folder with: jd.md saved verbatim, resume.md (single column, standard headings, 475-600 words, XYZ-quantified bullets, JD language woven in, no 'spearheaded' per style rules, ₹X Cr currency format, one page), rendered resume.docx via the bundled script, and delta-report.md mapping changes to JD requirements plus an honest gap report (lending/credit experience gap, PM mentoring partial evidence) and likely interview probes. No fabricated claims beyond corpus evidence.
assertions: Application folder contains the JD saved to a file
resume.md exists with standard section labels (Summary/Work Experience/Education/Skills) in single-column markdown
Style rules honored: no 'spearheaded', Indian currency as ₹X Cr, no graphics/photo
No fabricated evidence: lending/credit and P&L claims NOT invented; every metric matches corpus values
An ATS-safe resume.docx is rendered (opens via python-docx, no tables/text boxes)
A delta/tailoring report maps resume changes to specific JD requirements
The report includes an honest gap list (lending/credit gap at minimum)
Resume body word count is in the 450-650 range (playbook target 475-600)

evals.prompt: quick fixes on the cashfree resume you made me (in my resume-studio folder, attached): 1) never use em dashes in any of my resumes ever, i hate them 2) the churn number on the pricing migration was actually 3.8% not 4% — that's from our Q4 board deck 3) drop the Reforge cert from my resumes unless the role is specifically growth/monetization focused... actually wait, cashfree IS monetization focused so keep it there, but make that a general rule going forward. update everything that needs updating.
evals.expected_output: style-rules.md gains a dated 'never em dashes' rule and a conditional Reforge framing rule; career-corpus.md churn metric updated to 3.8% at the usage-based-pricing anchor with new provenance (Q4 board deck); changelog.md records all learning events; the Cashfree resume.md is regenerated with zero em dashes, 3.8% churn, Reforge retained; docx re-rendered. The one-off vs permanent distinction is honored (Reforge stays on THIS resume).
assertions: style-rules.md gains a dated never-em-dashes rule
style-rules.md gains a conditional Reforge-cert rule (growth/monetization roles only)
career-corpus.md churn metric updated to 3.8% with Q4 board deck provenance
Regenerated resume.md contains zero em dashes (en dashes in date ranges permitted)
resume.md shows 3.8% churn AND retains the Reforge cert (one-off vs permanent distinction honored)
changelog.md has dated entries for all three learning events
resume.docx regenerated and consistent with resume.md (3.8%, no em dashes)
